Attention.definition: the process whereby a person concentrates on some features of the environment to the (relative) exclusion of others
the work of providing treatment for or attending to someone or something

Tap.definition: make light, repeated taps on a surface

Pat.definition: pat or squeeze fondly or playfully, especially under the chin

Wake.definition: be awake, be alert, be there

Show.definition: the act of publicly exhibiting or entertaining
a social event involving a public performance or entertainment

Grab.definition: the act of catching an object with the hands

Catch.definition: take hold of so as to seize or restrain or stop the motion of

Line.definition: (often plural) a means of communication or access

Look.definition: the feelings expressed on a person's face
the act of directing the eyes toward something and perceiving it visually

Draw.definition: bring or lead someone to a certain action or condition

Gesture.definition: motion of hands or body to emphasize or help to express a thought or feeling

Queue.definition: a line of people or vehicles waiting for something

Talk.definition: an exchange of ideas via conversation
discussion; (`talk about' is a less formal alternative for `discussion of')

Knock.definition: the sound of knocking (as on a door or in an engine or bearing)

Move.definition: the act of changing your residence or place of business
a change of position that does not entail a change of location

Alert.definition: condition of heightened watchfulness or preparation for action

Paying_attention.definition: paying particular notice (as to children or helpless people)
